在.origin: A sprout 才 pushing up from the earth 土 — it shows where something is, right here at this spot.

写.origin: A little roof 冖 over your busy hand — under it you sit down and write your words.

sentence.en: I write my name in the notebook.

很.origin: The step radical 彳 walks beside 艮 — picture taking very, very many steps to mean a lot.

sentence.en: The weather is very nice today.

讲.origin: The speech radical 讠 next to a tidy well 井 — you talk and explain things in neat order.

sentence.en: The teacher is telling a story.

书.origin: Think of a pen making a flowing mark on paper — that writing becomes a book full of words.

朋.origin: Two moons 月 side by side, like two people walking together — that is what makes good friends.

友.origin: Two hands 又 reaching out and joining together — friends help each other, hand in hand.

sentence.en: Xiao Ming and I are friends.

午.origin: Picture the sun standing tall and straight overhead at the very middle of the day — that's noon.

里.origin: A field 田 sitting on the soil 土 — it marks the land inside, the place where things are kept.

sentence.en: The cat is inside the room.

不.origin: Picture a little bird flying up toward the sky line 一 but not landing — it means no, not yet.

再.origin: Think of stacking one thing on top of 一 another — doing it once more, again and again.

菜.origin: Grass 艹 growing above a picking hand 采 — you reach down and gather fresh vegetables to eat.

买.origin: Picture carrying coins to the market and bringing home a treasure — that is what it means to buy.

龙.origin: This whole character curls and twists like a long, magical dragon flying through the clouds.

打.origin: A hand 扌 swinging down on a nail 丁 — your hand taps, hits, or plays a game.

sentence.en: My little brother is playing basketball.

猫.origin: The animal radical 犭 beside a sprout 苗 — a small furry creature that says meow: a cat.

红.origin: The silk radical 纟 beside 工 — picture silk threads dyed a bright, warm red color.

绿.origin: The silk radical 纟 again — here the threads are dyed the fresh green of new leaves.

弟.origin: Picture cord wrapped neatly down a bow 弓, step by step — like a younger brother coming after.

sentence.en: My younger brother is very cute.

店.origin: A wide roof 广 sheltering a spot 占 — a cozy building where a shop sets up to sell things.

sentence.en: We go to the bookstore to buy books.

区.origin: An open box 匸 holding things inside — it marks off one small area or district from the rest.

饭.origin: The food radical 饣 beside 反 — it makes you think of a warm bowl of rice at mealtime.

钱.origin: The metal radical 钅 tells you it's made of metal — think of shiny metal coins, which are money.

听.origin: A mouth 口 sends out words and an ear nearby catches them — that is how you listen.

睡.origin: An eye 目 with lids drooping down 垂 — when your eyes hang heavy, it's time to sleep.

发.origin: Picture an arrow shooting out from a hand 又 — energy bursting out, sending something off.

sentence.en: The teacher hands out books to us.

诗.origin: The speech radical 讠 beside a temple 寺 — gentle, quiet words arranged into a beautiful poem.

草.origin: The grass radical 艹 sits above the early sun 早 — soft green grass waking up in the morning.

sentence.en: There are little flowers on the grass.

流.origin: Three water drops 氵 on the side — picture water flowing and streaming gently down a river.

sentence.en: The little river flows very fast.

美.origin: A person 大 wearing a grand woolly 羊 crown — standing tall and looking truly beautiful.

肉.origin: Picture lines of meat layered between an animal's ribs 冂 — that's where meat comes from.

份.origin: A person 亻 beside divide 分 — it's the share or portion that gets split and handed to you.

sentence.en: Give me a copy of the newspaper.

饺.origin: The food radical 饣 beside 交 — think of dough folded and crossed over to wrap a tasty dumpling.

sentence.en: We eat dumplings at New Year.

元.origin: A person 儿 with two strokes 二 marking the head on top — the first, the head, the main one.

时.origin: The sun 日 beside a measure 寸 — watching the sun move tells you what time it is.

sentence.en: It is time to go to school now.

动.origin: Clouds 云 swept along by strength 力 — when force pushes, things begin to move.

sentence.en: The little fish moves in the water.

踢.origin: The foot radical 足 on the left — your foot swings out to kick the ball away.

sentence.en: They are kicking a ball on the playground.

更.origin: Picture stacking one good thing over another — pushing it higher, making it even more.

sentence.en: Today is even colder than yesterday.

故.origin: An old thing 古 with a tapping hand 攵 — looking back at old times and the reasons behind them.

传.origin: A person 亻 beside 专 — picture handing something from person to person, passing it along.

sentence.en: Please pass the book to him.
